response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and backend engineer with a strong testing culture, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The most critical disqualifier is C++: the job requires 8 years of C++ experience, yet his resume shows roughly 6 months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project — and C++ does not even appear in his skills section. Beyond that, he has no demonstrated experience with cloud compute platforms or large-scale infrastructure at the required depth; his distributed systems work (Sociomantic) was in DLang, not C++, and focused on HTTP throughput rather than compute/storage architecture. He also lacks formal technical leadership of engineering teams, which is central to this role.
strengths: Genuine exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency design
Strong, demonstrated testing culture including TDD workshops, open-source testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and repeated mentoring/teaching
Broad polyglot experience and founding-level ownership of the Northwind Labs / GISCollective platform, showing end-to-end product delivery capability
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months) versus the 8-year minimum requirement; the language is absent from his own skills list
No experience with cloud compute platform internals, storage architecture, or hardware-level infrastructure — AWS CDK usage was limited to a pricing feature in a mail platform
No documented track record of leading or mentoring engineering teams; workshops indicate knowledge-sharing but not the sustained technical leadership this role demands
